title: Ustr Proposal More Clarity Or Not
section_path: Ustr Proposal More Clarity Or Not
section_path_text: Ustr Proposal More Clarity Or Not
summary:
Main
The Office of the United States Trade Representative (USTR), following extensive feedback from industry stakeholders, recently revised the Notice of Action and Proposed Action in Section 301 Investigation of China's Targeting the Maritime, Logistics, and Shipbuilding Sectors for Dominance on 17 April 2025. In a nutshell, under the revised terms, a vessel will now be subject to either: (a) one of the three fees directed under Annex I, II, or III; or (b) a requirement under Annex IV. In addition, there is a preferential order for applying these fees as follows: Annex IV > III > I > II, and on a non-cumulative basis.
